message:
fix(node): correct drain timeout audit ordering, starvation threshold, and error code format

Cancellation protocol and cancellable task runtime both had a subtle
ordering bug: on drain timeout without force mode, the audit event was
recorded after the early-return error, so the timeout was never
captured in the audit trail. Move audit emission before the error return
in both code paths.

Control lane policy emitted starvation events every time a lane's
zero-tick counter was reset, even if the counter was below the
configured threshold. Add threshold gate so events only fire when
the starvation threshold is actually reached.

Error code constants in cancellable_task used underscores (ERR_CXT_...)
but the project convention uses dashes (ERR-CXT_...). Align to convention.

Lockstep harness timeout handling refactored to always join reader
threads (preventing resource leaks) and produce a distinct TIMEOUT
exit marker instead of bailing before capturing output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
